summary: Never-filled PENDING suggestions are expired to EXPIRED, which calibration_engine counts as closed losses, contradicting the honest fill model.
impact: Setups whose limit entries were never touched accumulate phantom losses in empirical winRate, depressing calibrated confidence and skewing suggestion ranking for otherwise healthy setups.
fix: The proposed fix is directionally right but incomplete. (1) In both expire functions, split the single UPDATE into two: rows with status PENDING → set a new status "MISSED"; rows with status ACTIVE → keep "EXPIRED". (2) Do NOT change CLOSED_STATUSES semantics only by adding MISSED-exclusion — MISSED simply never enters CLOSED_STATUSES since it isn't added; also leave the gap-through case at accuracy_tracker.ts:82 as EXPIRED only if the intent is to keep pessimistic accounting, otherwise mark it MISSED too (it also never filled; comment says "missed trade"). (3) Add "MISSED" to the API status enum (backend/src/schemas — SuggestionStatus is orval-generated, so update the OpenAPI spec/zod source and regenerate) and to any UI/status filters (routes/suggestions.ts history filters, frontend badge rendering); the DB column is varchar(20) so no migration needed. (4) Audit other consumers of EXPIRED (learning_engine.ts, post_market.ts, routes/system.ts) so MISSED rows aren't silently dropped from views that should show them.
appliedFix: expireOldSuggestions/expireTodayIntraday split: PENDING rows -> status MISSED, ACTIVE rows -> EXPIRED (shared expiryConditions() helper). MISSED added to WS broadcast cast, all 6 status enums in backend/src/schemas/api.ts, suggestionStatus.ts, getSuggestionHistoryStatus.ts. Gap-through case at accuracy_tracker checkSuggestionOutcomes already writes MISSED.

summary: Downtrend+elevated-VIX long block is a no-op: the if-block only logs 'Skipping long generation' and filters nothing.
impact: BUY suggestions are generated into TRENDING_DOWN regimes with VIX>18 — exactly the regime the gate was written to avoid — while logs falsely claim the gate fired; summarizeRejections even lists the never-emitted reason downtrend_vix_long_block.
fix: The proposed fix is correct in substance; the better placement is the signal loop in generateSuggestions (generator.ts:618-637) rather than ingestSignal, because addRejection/rejectionCounts are local to that function and that is what feeds summarizeRejections. Concretely, capture the block condition where it's already computed: at line 438 set `const longBlock = regime === "TRENDING_DOWN" && !!state.indiaVix && state.indiaVix > 18;` (keep the log inside the if), then in the loop before calling ingestSignal add: `if (longBlock && signal.signal === "BUY") { addRejection("downtrend_vix_long_block"); continue; }`. If the gate should also protect the standalone ingestSignal entry point (it is exported and called elsewhere), duplicate the check there reading getMarketState(), but the loop-level check is what makes the already-listed rejection reason actually flow into diagnostics.
appliedFix: Captured longBlock at regime check; signal loop now rejects BUY signals with addRejection("downtrend_vix_long_block") when TRENDING_DOWN and VIX>18.

summary: maxSectorExposure and maxSameDirectionOpenPositions caps are configured and bookkept (sectorCounts fetched and incremented) but never checked anywhere.
impact: Portfolio can concentrate all open suggestions in one sector or one direction, violating the configured risk limits silently; the sector_cap/direction_cap rejection buckets can never fire.
fix: The proposed fix is directionally right for the batch loop but incomplete. In the generator loop (generator.ts:618-637): seed per-direction counts from openSuggestions (already fetched at line 358) alongside the existing sectorCounts (already seeded from getOpenSectorCounts), and before ingestSignal reject when sectorCounts[sector] >= cfg.maxSectorExposure (addRejection("sector_cap")) or directionCounts[signal.signal] >= cfg.maxSameDirectionOpenPositions (addRejection(signal.signal === "BUY" ? "direction_cap_buy" : "direction_cap_sell")); increment both counters on success. Additionally — to cover the realtime orchestrator path which bypasses assessRisk — either enforce the same DB-backed cap check inside ingestSignal itself (query ACTIVE/PENDING counts by sector/direction near the existing duplicate check at generator.ts:792), or route orchestrator signals through assessRisk before ingestSignal.
appliedFix: Batch loop enforces maxSectorExposure/maxSameDirectionOpenPositions with sector_cap/direction_cap_* rejections and counter increments; ingestSignal also enforces DB-backed sector/direction caps for the realtime path.

summary: Realtime orchestrator path calls ingestSignal without the maxOpenPositions check and without the VIX/breadth-adjusted minRequiredRR (defaults to 1.3).
impact: Open positions can exceed cfg.maxOpenPositions without bound via the realtime path, and lower-R:R trades are admitted precisely in high-VIX/weak-breadth regimes where the scheduler path raises the floor to up to 1.75.
fix: In ingestSignal (backend/src/suggestions/generator.ts), before the insert: (1) query count of suggestions with status in ("ACTIVE","PENDING") and reject if >= getConfig().maxOpenPositions; (2) compute the dynamic floor from getMarketState() (base 1.3, +0.2 if indiaVix > 16, +0.25 if declineCount > advanceCount * 1.5) and use `options?.minRequiredRR ?? dynamicFloor` so the scheduler's explicit value still takes precedence. Keep the existing early-exit check in generateSuggestions (line 363) for slotsAvailable/efficiency rather than removing it. Note the new count check must run before the ltp-gated RR block since ltp may be undefined when fetchLTPForSymbols fails.
appliedFix: ingestSignal now checks open ACTIVE/PENDING count against cfg.maxOpenPositions before insert and defaults minRequiredRR to the VIX/breadth-adjusted dynamic floor (options.minRequiredRR still takes precedence).

summary: Calibrated confidence is applied in ingestSignal AFTER signals are ranked by raw model confidence and after position sizing, so it never affects which suggestions are selected or how large they are.
impact: Signals are sorted at signal_generator.ts:634 by uncalibrated confidence and the ingest loop breaks at slotsAvailable (generator.ts:619); setups with empirically weak win rates consume the limited daily slots ahead of empirically strong ones. The entire calibration system only changes the displayed number — zero effect on suggestion selection accuracy.
fix: In runIntelligencePipeline (backend/src/analysis/signal_generator.ts), after final confidence is computed for each signal (and tradeType is determinable), call calibrateConfidence(signal.confidence, setupType, tradeType) and assign the blended value to signal.confidence BEFORE the sort at line 634, so ranking and the slotsAvailable loop in generator.ts use the outcome-blended score. Then remove (or guard) the calibrateConfidence call in ingestSignal (generator.ts:827) to avoid double-blending, since the blend is not idempotent. Note the bulk-deal ±5 adjustment in ingestSignal (generator.ts:754) currently precedes calibration; either move it before calibration in the pipeline or accept it as a post-calibration nudge. Making risk_engine position sizing consume calibrated confidence is a separate enhancement — sizing does not currently use confidence at all.
appliedFix: calibrateConfidence applied to all pipeline signals in generateSuggestionsFromWatchlist BEFORE re-sorting by confidence and slot selection; ingestSignal skips re-blending via confidenceCalibrated flag (realtime path still calibrates). Sizing doesn't consume confidence today, per the vetted fix note.

summary: winRate = wins/total counts EXPIRED rows as losses, including PENDING suggestions whose entry was never touched (expired by accuracy_tracker.ts:82/214/250), contradicting the 'honest fill model' that promises never-filled trades don't count.
impact: Every setup's empirical win rate is systematically deflated by phantom trades that never filled; via the blend at line 215 this drags calibrated confidence down for all future signals, and phantom rows also inflate `samples`, activating the n>=10 blend gate on fake evidence.
fix: Proposed fix is essentially correct, with one refinement: filled-but-timed-out trades (ACTIVE → EXPIRED) also have pnlInr = null, so the minimal filter `if (row.status === 'EXPIRED' && row.pnlInr == null) continue;` in refreshCalibration's row loop drops those too — it cannot "keep truly-filled expiries" as the optional variant suggests. Distinguishing never-filled from filled-expired requires a schema change (e.g., a wasFilled/everActive boolean set when a PENDING row is promoted to ACTIVE, or expiry recording mark-to-market pnl). The minimal filter is still the right immediate fix and matches refreshSetupDemotions' existing behavior.
appliedFix: CLOSED_STATUSES in calibration_engine.ts unchanged (no MISSED added) — MISSED rows never enter winRate. Root cause fixed via the expiry split in accuracy_tracker.ts (see finding 0).

summary: The 4h timeframe is永 always a daily-candle proxy because the real-4h threshold (hourlyCandles.length >= 180) is unreachable when fetchHourlyCandles is called with daysBack=14 (~70 candles max).
impact: MTF confluence double-counts the daily trend (proxy-4h w=1.4 + daily w=2 + weekly-from-daily w=2.4 of 6.8 total), inflating confluenceScore and MTF boosts; hasStrongHigherTfContext is always true so the WEAK_HTF_CONTEXT_PENALTY and the 62-point confluence cap are dead code, and every ScanResult falsely reports higherTfReliability:'full'. Suggestions get systematically over-scored on single-timeframe evidence.
fix: The proposed fix is directionally correct but incomplete on two points. (1) Increase hourly lookback: change both call sites (stock_scanner.ts:1470 and :1706) to `fetchHourlyCandles(stock.key, 40, ...)` (~28 trading days × ~7 candles ≈ 190–196 ≥ 180), or lower the threshold at :1482 to something reachable with 14 days (e.g. `>= 56`, ≈14 real 4h bars) — but note `analyzeMultiTimeframeFromData` and the `tf4hEffective.length >= 30` gate need ≥30 4h bars for `hasStrongHigherTfContext`, so with a lowered threshold you'd also need to relax that gate or the real-4h path would flip hasStrongHigherTfContext false and return null via hasUsableHigherTf. The daysBack=40 approach is safer. (2) Decouple the two flags: set `const usedProxy4h = hourlyCandles.length < 180;` then `hasStrongHigherTfContext = !usedProxy4h && tf4hEffective.length >= 30 && tfWeeklyEffective.length >= 30`, while keeping `hasUsableHigherTf` as-is (proxy still usable) so the existing haircut/62-cap/'fallback' paths actually engage instead of hard-dropping. Apply the same change to the second occurrence around :1706-1713. Also note `fetchHourlyCandles`'s cache key includes daysBack, so changing the constant won't collide with stale cached entries.
appliedFix: Increased fetchHourlyCandles daysBack 14→40 at both call sites (~190 hourly candles ≥ 180 threshold) and decoupled the flags: hasStrongHigherTfContext now requires real (non-proxy) 4h data via usedProxy4h, while hasUsableHigherTf keeps accepting the daily-proxy fallback so the haircut/62-cap/'fallback' reliability paths engage.

summary: aggregateDailyToWeekly computes the weekday with getUTCDay() on IST (+05:30) timestamps, so Monday IST candles (Sunday 18:30 UTC) are bucketed into the previous week's bar.
impact: Every weekly OHLC bar is wrong: the prior week's close becomes Monday's close and its high/low absorb Monday's range. Weekly trend, EMA order, and strength in multi_timeframe (weight 2.4, plus the BUY/SELL veto on weeklyTrend) are computed on distorted bars, skewing every suggestion that relies on weekly context.
fix: Use the first proposed variant only: shift into IST before extracting the weekday — `const d = new Date(new Date(c.timestamp).getTime() + 330*60*1000);` then keep the existing getUTCDay/setUTCDate/setUTCHours logic. Do NOT use the alternative of bucketing by the date portion of the timestamp string: for the multi_timeframe.fetchCandles path the string is a DB-roundtripped toISOString() value ("2026-07-12T18:30:00.000Z" for Monday IST), whose date portion is the previous UTC day (Sunday), which would reintroduce the same off-by-one for that caller.
appliedFix: aggregateDailyToWeekly now shifts timestamps by +5:30 (330 min) before getUTCDay(), so Monday IST candles (Sunday 18:30 UTC) bucket into the correct week.

summary: fetchCandles only fetches new data when lastCachedDateStr < toDateStr, so for 60minute candles no API call is made after the first fetch of the day — intraday data is frozen at whatever hour it was first cached; it also never backfills when the DB cache is missing the older part of the requested range.
impact: The intraday scanner (analyzeMultiTimeframe → fetchCandles '60minute') runs all day on stale hourly candles presented as fresh — crossover1h, hourlyConfirm, and volume signals reflect the morning, not now. The missing-backfill case silently returns a truncated series (e.g. 20 days when 180 were requested), degrading daily-TF analysis.
fix: Direction is right but needs two adjustments. (1) Tail freshness: in the else-branch, also fetch when lastCachedDateStr === toDateStr and the interval is intraday ("60minute"/"240minute"/other *minute) and Date.now() - lastCachedTime.getTime() exceeds the interval length (or a staleness threshold, e.g. 15 min); use fromDate = lastCachedDateStr for the delta — the onConflictDoUpdate upsert already handles the overlapping day. Note the upstox client memory/disk cache keys on (instrument|interval|toDate|fromDate) with a 10-min TTL (upstox-client.ts:519,543), so identical intra-day delta requests are still coalesced to at most one API call per 10 min — acceptable, but don't set the staleness threshold below cacheTimeMs or the check is a no-op. (2) Head backfill: do NOT compare earliest cached timestamp strictly against fromDateTime — fromDateStr is calendar-days-back, so the earliest candle legitimately lands days later (weekends/holidays); a strict comparison would trigger a spurious full refetch on nearly every call. Use a tolerance (e.g. earliest cached candle more than ~5-7 calendar days after fromDateStr) before fetching the head range [fromDateStr, earliestCachedDateStr], and upsert as with the tail.
appliedFix: fetchCandles now refetches the current day's intraday candles when lastCachedDateStr === toDateStr and the last cached candle is >15 min old (≥ upstox client's 10-min TTL), and backfills the head range when the earliest cached candle is >7 calendar days after fromDateStr; both delta fetches go through the existing onConflictDoUpdate upsert.

summary: Unconditional dereference of aiResult.technicalRanking.bullish_probability throws for legacy 'kronos'-shaped results that the filter at line 197 deliberately admits, and the throw inside the try discards the entire AI batch.
impact: Whole ranking cycles silently degrade to deterministicFallback — all AI composite scores and RL predictions already computed are thrown away with no log, materially lowering suggestion ranking quality.
fix: Use the same coalesce as line 197: const ranking = aiResult.technicalRanking ?? (aiResult as any).kronos; then render ranking?.bullish_probability != null ? (...) : 'N/A'. Also log err in the catch at line 261.
appliedFix: Guarded legacy-shape deref: coalesce technicalRanking ?? kronos, render 'N/A' when bullish_probability missing, and log the batch-catch error via console.error (worker has no logger import).

summary: initTickFeeder has no reentrancy guard: concurrent calls (triggered by overlapping syncMonitoredSubscriptions from WS handlers) interleave across its awaits, overwriting eventBusUnsubscribe and leaking a live duplicate marketTick handler.
impact: Duplicate handlers double-process every tick against the shared subscriptions map — tick history duplicated, tickDistribution.ingestTick fired twice, sector-rotation volume flow double-counted — skewing volume/momentum inputs to candidate detection; leak compounds with each resync.
fix: The proposed serialization is correct and sufficient: chain inits via a module-level promise (`initPromise = initPromise.then(() => doInit(stocks)); return initPromise;`) inside initTickFeeder so stopTickFeeder always runs after the previous init has assigned its unsubscribers. Better still, put the mutex in syncMonitoredSubscriptions since it also races on upstoxConnectionManager.updateSubscriptions. Note the "unsubscribe previous handler synchronously before the first await" part of the proposed fix already happens (stopTickFeeder at line 58 is before any await) — the leak comes from two in-flight calls both seeing eventBusUnsubscribe === null, which only serialization (or a generation counter checked before the line-94 subscribe) fixes. Downgrade severity: the line-115 unchanged-tick guard prevents the claimed double-processing; this is a listener/memory leak, not a data-skew bug.
appliedFix: Serialized initTickFeeder through a module-level initPromise chain (failure-tolerant via .catch) delegating to doInitTickFeeder, so overlapping inits can't leak duplicate marketTick handlers.

summary: Missing tick volume is replaced with a fabricated hardcoded 100000 (and || also swallows a legitimate 0), which is persisted and fed to tickDistribution and sector flow.
impact: Fake volume propagates to candidate detection's relativeVolume = volume/previousVolume, fabricating or masking 'relative volume' signals and polluting sector rotation with invented turnover — directly corrupts suggestion inputs.
fix: In tick_feeder.ts:111 use `const volume = tickEvent.volume ?? sub.volume;` (sub.volume is a non-nullable number initialized to 0, so no third fallback needed). Downstream, treat volume 0 as unknown: intelligence_worker already guards with `previousVolume > 0 ? ... : 1` so relativeVolume degrades safely, but also skip/short-circuit updateSectorFlowFromTick when volume === 0 so the sector-flow volume EMA isn't seeded with 0 either (currently it would set ema=0 on first tick); e.g. `if (volume <= 0) return;` at the top of updateSectorFlowFromTick, or only call it from tick_feeder when volume > 0. Additionally consider changing the dedupe check at line 115 so price-only ticks (volume unknown) still pass through when price changed — the existing `lastPrice === sub.lastPrice && volume === sub.volume` already handles that correctly.
appliedFix: Replaced fabricated 100000 fallback with `tickEvent.volume ?? sub.volume` (carries last real volume, honest 0 otherwise) and skipped updateSectorFlowFromTick when volume <= 0 so the flow EMA isn't seeded with unknown volume.

summary: Geopolitical/political keyword scoring uses raw substring matching (`keyword in text_lower`) with no word boundaries, so 'war' matches 'warns/award/toward', 'loc' matches 'local/unlock', 'modi' matches 'commodity', 'gst' matches arbitrary words.
impact: Innocuous headlines ('RBI warns banks...', 'commodity prices rise') get spurious keyword hits that are blended at 40% weight into world_score and india_political_score, which together carry 45% of the sentiment composite. This injects a persistent false negative bias into every symbol's composite score and can falsely trigger the -15 point macro crash penalty in _compute_composite_score (main.py:504) when world_score < -0.5.
fix: Proposed fix is correct; minor refinement: compile once at module load for both dicts, e.g. `_KEYWORD_PATTERNS = {kw: (re.compile(r'\b' + re.escape(kw) + r'\b'), impact) for kw, impact in {**INDIA_GEOPOLITICAL_KEYWORDS, **INDIA_POLITICAL_KEYWORDS}.items()}` and use `pattern.search(text_lower)` in _score_geopolitical_impact (note the merged dict would drop duplicate keys — keep two pattern dicts if both lists must be scored independently, since a keyword appearing in both should count twice as today). Word-bounded 'war', 'modi', 'gst' become safe; 'loc' should be replaced with 'line of control' (or matched case-sensitively as 'LoC') since word-bounded lowercase 'loc' still barely occurs as a standalone word but the acronym is normally written 'LoC' and lowercasing destroys that signal.
appliedFix: Precompiled word-boundary regex patterns (two dicts kept so dual-listed keywords still count twice); replaced bare 'loc' with 'loc violation' + 'line of control'. Verified 'warns'/'commodity'/'unlock' no longer match.

summary: For volatile stocks (vol > 2%) `closes` is replaced by a lagging EMA-3 series before inference, and forecast_return_pct is then computed against the smoothed last close (line 134 / 200) instead of the actual last traded price.
impact: Exactly for high-volatility candidates, after a sharp move the EMA base lags the real price (e.g. after a 5% drop the smoothed base sits well above market), so forecast_return_pct is systematically biased. That biased return feeds the sigmoid in _compute_composite_score (main.py:485) worth up to 30/100 points, skewing the ranking of the most volatile names, and the returned median_forecast prices are inconsistent with the caller's real last close.
fix: Proposed fix is correct. Simplest implementation: in infer(), capture `actual_last = float(closes[-1])` before the smoothing block, pass the smoothed series to _infer_with_model/_infer_fallback as context only, then recompute `ret_pct = ((result.median_forecast[-1] - actual_last) / actual_last) * 100` and the trend label from it on the returned ChronosResult — fixing both paths in one place. (Note the returned median_forecast quantile prices remain in smoothed space; if callers need price-consistent forecasts, optionally rebase them by the ratio actual_last / smoothed_last.)
appliedFix: infer() captures actual_last before EMA smoothing, rebases median/quantile forecasts from smoothed to actual price space, and recomputes forecast_return_pct + trend against the actual last traded close for both model and fallback paths.

summary: Live candle's volume bar is set to the cumulative DAILY volume from the tick feed, not the per-bar volume, so the last bar's volume histogram is wrong by orders of magnitude.
impact: Backend ticks carry `volume` extracted from Upstox's 1d OHLC (connection_manager.ts:326 — daily cumulative volume). PriceChart writes `Math.max(lastCandle.volume, data.volume || 0)` into a single 1-minute/15-minute bar. During market hours the newest bar shows e.g. 4,000,000 (day total) next to bars of ~5,000, crushing the volume pane's scale — visibly wrong data in a live demo, and it misleads anyone reading volume confirmation for a signal.
fix: In PriceChart.tsx:755 replace the value expression with `value: isOpenNewBar ? 1 : lastCandle.volume` — use `1` (not `0`) as the new-bar placeholder to match the file's existing live-bar volume placeholder convention (lines 442 and 467), and let the 60s refetch backfill real per-bar volume. Optionally skip the correction for `activeTf.interval === "day"`, where the daily cumulative volume is actually the correct per-bar value: `value: activeTf.interval === "day" ? Math.max(lastCandle.volume, data.volume || 0) : (isOpenNewBar ? 1 : lastCandle.volume)`.
appliedFix: PriceChart live-bar volume = delta of cumulative daily volume since bar open (re-based per bar and on counter reset); day interval keeps cumulative value.

summary: LivePrice (and LiveChangePct.tsx:34 identically) injects its stale `fallback` prop into the global marketDataStore via `updateFromRest`, so a snapshot price captured at suggestion-generation time is republished app-wide as a live 'rest' quote.
impact: SuggestionsSlider passes `fallback={currentPrice}` where `currentPrice` can be `s.currentPrice || s.outcomePrice` — a value frozen when the suggestion row was fetched. `updateFromRest` stores it with `source: 'rest'` and notifies ALL subscribers for that symbol, so every LivePrice/LiveChangePct/PriceChart consumer of that symbol now renders the stale number as if it were a fresh quote (PriceChart even uses `liveData?.ltp` as the AI-target base at PriceChart.tsx:639). A price/changePct pair can also become internally inconsistent (price from one baseline, change% from another).
fix: The proposed fix is correct and sufficient: delete the `marketDataStore.updateFromRest(...)` call (and the surrounding `if (ltp == null && fallback != null)` block) from both LivePrice.tsx and LiveChangePct.tsx. Local rendering is already handled by `displayPrice = ltp ?? fallback` / `displayChange = changePct ?? fallback`. One behavior note: other consumers of the same symbol that pass no fallback (e.g. PriceChart) will no longer see the seeded value — PriceChart correctly falls back to `forecast.lastClose || lastClose`, which is the desired behavior.
appliedFix: Removed marketDataStore.updateFromRest(fallback) seeding from both LivePrice and LiveChangePct; fallback is now render-only.

summary: LTP fetch failure is swallowed (.catch(() => ({}))), and ingestSignal skips the late-entry and live R:R gates entirely when ltp is missing.
impact: During any Upstox outage, signals priced off stale daily candles are inserted PENDING with no chase/R:R verification, then auto-promoted to ACTIVE at whatever price the market has moved to — polluting stats and suggesting already-run entries.
fix: The proposed per-signal rejection is the right shape, but note the outer `.catch` at line 616 is not the root cause — fetchLTPForSymbols already returns {} on every failure path (no token, no keys, API error), so removing the .catch alone changes nothing. Fix in the signal loop (generator.ts:618-637): before calling ingestSignal, check `const ltp = livePrices[signal.symbol]; if (!ltp || ltp <= 0) { addRejection("ltp_unavailable"); continue; }` so the rejection surfaces in rejectionCounts/lastGenerationDiagnostics. Alternatively (or additionally, to cover the other caller at intelligence/orchestrator.ts:212 which has the same hole), make ingestSignal itself reject when livePrice is missing instead of silently skipping the gates — the `if (ltp && ltp > 0)` at line 760 becomes `if (!ltp || ltp <= 0) return false;` with a warn log. Keep fetchLTPForSymbols's empty-map-on-error contract (runOutcomeCheck at line 262 already handles {} correctly).
appliedFix: Loop rejects missing/zero LTP with addRejection("ltp_unavailable"); ingestSignal hard-rejects when livePrice is absent instead of skipping late-entry/live-R:R gates, covering the orchestrator caller.

summary: PENDING promotion keeps the planned entryPrice even when the polled price has already gapped most of the way to target, so later target hits book profit a real stop-order fill would not capture.
impact: Win PnL and winRate are systematically overstated for gappy fills (the EXPIRED guard only covers gaps fully through target), inflating calibration and confidence for affected setups.
fix: Directionally right but incomplete as stated: `promotions` is a string[] of ids and the promotion is one batch UPDATE (lines 147-152), so there is nowhere to put the per-row fill price. Change `promotions` to `Array<{ id: string; fillPrice: number }>` where fillPrice = suggestion.direction === "BUY" ? Math.max(entry, price) : Math.min(entry, price) (given the touched check, this is just `price`), then perform per-row updates: .set({ status: "ACTIVE", entryPrice: fillPrice.toFixed(2) }). Note the watermark logic (lines 95-102) seeds highestPrice/lowestPrice from entryPrice, so updating entryPrice at promotion also keeps MFE/MAE baselines consistent with the modeled fill. Optionally recompute quantity if position sizing was based on entry-to-stop risk, but at minimum the entryPrice update fixes the PnL overstatement.
appliedFix: PENDING promotion models fill at observed (possibly gapped) price: fillPrice = max(entry, price) for BUY / min for SELL, written to entryPrice on promotion; gapped-past-target case closed as MISSED with null pnl.

summary: computeAdaptiveConfidence ('confidence_v1' + shadow model) is exported but never called anywhere in the codebase — the sentiment/volume/sector-aware confidence model is dead code.
impact: Whatever product intent this model carried (sentiment boost, volume confirmation, sector alignment, EV penalty) silently does not run; anyone reading modelVersion 'confidence_v1' in the output type will assume it's live. Its internal math is also wrong if ever wired in: rawWinRate (line 66) is over decided trades only but is weighted by sampleSize including EXPIRED (line 70), and the 'recency-weighted, half-life of 30 trades' comment (line 67) describes weighting that does not exist.
fix: Either delete computeAdaptiveConfidence or wire it into the pipeline; if kept, weight rawWinRate by `decided` (wins+losses) not sampleSize, and implement or remove the recency-weighting comment.
appliedFix: computeAdaptiveConfidence and its ConfidenceInput/Output types deleted from confidence_engine.ts (zero callers confirmed). File now only exports evaluateAutomationHealth.

summary: evaluateAutomationHealth computes winRate over all closed rows including EXPIRED, so forced end-of-day intraday expiries (expireTodayIntraday) count as non-wins and can trip the winRate<40% auto-pause.
impact: A run of scratch trades (expired at close, ~0 PnL, never hit stop) combined with a couple of small losers can falsely pause all suggestion generation via monitorAutomationHealth (jobs.ts:404), halting the product on non-degraded performance.
fix: In evaluateAutomationHealth: `const losses = rows.filter(r => r.status === "STOP_HIT").length; const decided = wins + losses; if (decided < 12) return { shouldPause: false, winRate: 0, avgPnl: 0, trades, reason: null }; const winRate = (wins / decided) * 100;` — i.e. the proposed fix plus explicitly defining losses as STOP_HIT. Optionally also exclude EXPIRED rows that were never filled (former PENDING) from avgPnl, since they were never real trades; at minimum keep avgPnl over filled trades only.
appliedFix: evaluateAutomationHealth CLOSED_STATUSES excludes MISSED (never-filled rows can no longer trip the winRate<40% auto-pause); comment documents EXPIRED = filled-but-timed-out only.

summary: fetchCandles builds day bounds with UTC suffixes (fromDateStr+'T00:00:00Z', toDateStr+'T23:59:59Z'), but candle timestamps are IST — the window is shifted 5.5h: the daily candle for toDate+1 (stamped toDate 18:30 UTC) is included and fromDate's own candle is excluded.
impact: One-day lookahead bias whenever a historical toDate is passed (backtests, post-market scans with toDateOverride): the analysis 'sees' the next day's candle, making backtested suggestion accuracy look better than live performance; live scans also drop the first day of the window.
fix: Use the existing IST helpers: `const { start } = getISTDayBounds(fromDateStr); const { end } = getISTDayBounds(toDateStr);` from lib/ist-time.ts instead of constructing Z-suffixed Dates.
appliedFix: fetchCandles day bounds now use getISTDayBounds(fromDateStr).start / getISTDayBounds(toDateStr).end instead of Z-suffixed dates, eliminating the 5.5h window shift (one-day lookahead on historical toDate, dropped first day live).

summary: Missing `else` in the FII flow modifier lets a single condition apply two upgrades: SIDEWAYS_RANGE + FII>2000 becomes BULLISH_STEADY and then immediately BULLISH_EXPANSION; symmetrically SIDEWAYS + FII<-2000 becomes BEARISH_CONTRACTION and triggers the suggestion pause on a flat market.
impact: A flat, range-bound day is classified as the strongest trend regime purely on one day of FII flow, activating the wrong scanners; on the bearish side it hard-pauses all suggestions ('FII net selling — high risk') on a sideways market, killing valid signals.
fix: Make the second upgrade conditional on the regime having been BULLISH_STEADY/BEARISH_STEADY before the modifier ran: change `if (regime === "BULLISH_STEADY")` at line 288 to `else if`, and likewise `if (regime === "BEARISH_STEADY")` at line 304.
appliedFix: Made both FII upgrade/downgrade second steps `else if` so the modifier applies at most one regime step (SIDEWAYS+FII no longer jumps two steps to EXPANSION/CONTRACTION).

summary: Eviction check in subscribe_symbol old-symbol cleanup (and the close handler at line 338) only inspects other clients' activeSymbol, ignoring subscribedSymbols batch subscriptions, unlike the correct check in unsubscribe_symbol.
impact: A symbol another client still watches via subscribe_symbols/watchlist gets removeManualMonitoredSymbol'd — its Upstox feed is torn down and that client's ticks silently stop.
fix: Use the same predicate as unsubscribe_symbol in both places: tcClient.activeSymbol === oldSymbol || tcClient.subscribedSymbols?.has(oldSymbol).
appliedFix: Factored shared isSymbolStillNeeded(symbol, exceptClient) helper checking activeSymbol AND subscribedSymbols; used in subscribe_symbol old-symbol cleanup, unsubscribe_symbol, and close handler.

summary: broadcastFn never checks tc.isAuthenticated, and every connection gets default topics {suggestions, alerts} before auth, so unauthenticated remote clients receive broadcasts during the 5-second auth window.
impact: Trade suggestions and alerts (core product output) are streamed to unauthenticated remote connections for up to 5 seconds per connection attempt.
fix: In broadcastFn, add the auth check to the per-client guard and include the flag in the cast: change line 400's cast to include `isAuthenticated?: boolean`, and at line 401 use `if (tc.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN && tc.isAuthenticated) { ... }`. Local and auth-disabled clients are already set isAuthenticated=true at connection (line 105), so they are unaffected. Optionally apply the same guard in broadcastToClient for defense in depth (its only current caller sends a harmless pong).
appliedFix: broadcastFn now requires tc.isAuthenticated (cast includes isAuthenticated?: boolean); default {suggestions, alerts} topics assigned only after auth succeeds (local/auth-disabled at connect, remote after token auth). broadcastToClient left unguarded intentionally — its only caller is the initial connection pong, which the finding notes is harmless and would break pre-auth handshake if gated.

summary: unsubscribe_symbol only trims the symbol while subscribe paths uppercase, so case-mismatched unsubscribes fail to clear activeSymbol/subscribedSymbols and corrupt the cross-client otherSubscribed comparison.
impact: Client keeps receiving ticks for a symbol it unsubscribed from, and backend eviction decisions run against the wrong key, leaving stale manual subscriptions.
fix: Normalize with .trim().toUpperCase() in unsubscribe_symbol, identical to subscribe_symbol.
appliedFix: Single normalizeSymbol() (trim+uppercase) used by subscribe_symbol, unsubscribe_symbol, and batch subscribe_symbols/subscribe_watchlist; batch path now also sends normalized symbols to addManualMonitoredSymbols.

summary: _market_cache (line 20) is declared but never used; the comment 'Market-wide: use cached if fresh (same for all symbols)' is not implemented — every cache-miss symbol refetches the same 6 market/world/political RSS feeds and reruns FinBERT on them.
impact: In one batch ranking pass, different symbols get different market_wide/india_political/world scores whenever a shared feed intermittently fails for some fetches (0.0 vs real score = up to 0.7 of composite weight differs), making cross-symbol ranking inconsistent within the same batch. With feeds down, each symbol burns up to ~10s in retry/timeout (2 attempts x 5s x 7 feeds), so a 200-candidate batch at semaphore 4 can take minutes and time out upstream.
fix: Actually use _market_cache: key the combined (market_wide_score, india_political_score, world_score) under a shared key with CACHE_TTL_SEC; on cache hit skip fetching/scoring the 6 shared feeds and only fetch+score the per-symbol Yahoo feed. Guard with an asyncio.Lock to avoid thundering-herd refresh.
appliedFix: Implemented _get_market_wide_scores(): the 6 shared market/political/world feeds are fetched+scored once under an asyncio.Lock (double-checked) and cached in _market_cache with CACHE_TTL_SEC; per-symbol Yahoo feed fetched separately. Verified 6 shared fetches for 2 symbols with identical shared scores.

summary: Single-inference async handlers run blocking model inference directly on the event loop: infer_technical_ranking (line 438, pandas/ta), infer_chronos (line 458, torch forward pass), and predict_rl (line 653, rl_agent_service.predict).
impact: One Chronos torch inference (hundreds of ms on CPU) or RL prediction stalls the entire event loop, delaying concurrent /inference/batch progress and /health responses; under load the Node backend sees timeouts and falls back, degrading suggestion freshness. The batch endpoint already uses asyncio.to_thread, so these paths are inconsistently unsafe.
fix: Proposed fix is correct; complete forms: in infer_technical_ranking use `result = await asyncio.to_thread(technical_pattern_engine.infer, req.ohlcv, req.features or {})`; in infer_chronos use `result = await asyncio.to_thread(chronos_service.infer, req.closes, req.steps or 5)`; in predict_rl use `result = await asyncio.to_thread(rl_agent_service.predict, df, macro_data)`.
appliedFix: Wrapped technical_pattern_engine.infer, chronos_service.infer, and rl_agent_service.predict in asyncio.to_thread in their single-inference handlers.

summary: analyze_sentiment calls init_models() synchronously inside an async function; if a request arrives before the startup loader thread (main.py:283) reaches sentiment init, the FinBERT download/load runs on the event loop.
impact: Loading ProsusAI/finbert (possibly a multi-hundred-MB download on first run) freezes the entire FastAPI event loop — /health and all inference endpoints hang for the duration, upstream health checks fail, and the whole batch scoring stalls.
fix: The proposed fix works but pick the second variant, or combine them: change lines 298-299 to `if sentiment_pipeline is None and not _sentiment_initialized: await asyncio.to_thread(init_models)`. Note the idempotence guard is check-then-set without a lock, so `asyncio.to_thread(init_models)` racing the startup loader thread could double-load the pipeline if both pass the `if _sentiment_initialized` check before either sets it; to be safe, add a `threading.Lock` around the body of init_models (or simply return the neutral dict when `not _sentiment_initialized` and let the startup thread finish, accepting neutral sentiment during warm-up).
appliedFix: analyze_sentiment now does `await asyncio.to_thread(init_models)`; init_models body guarded by a module-level threading.Lock making it idempotent against the startup loader thread race.

summary: `asyncio.create_task(asyncio.to_thread(save_to_db))` stores no reference to the task; per asyncio documentation, unreferenced tasks can be garbage-collected before completion.
impact: Sentiment snapshots are silently dropped from fundamental_snapshots, so PIT backtest queries (main.py:558-564, filed_date <= as_of_date) find no row and use 0.0 sentiment — backtests silently diverge from live scoring, invalidating strategy validation of the sentiment component.
fix: Keep a module-level `_bg_tasks: set = set()`; `t = asyncio.create_task(asyncio.to_thread(save_to_db)); _bg_tasks.add(t); t.add_done_callback(_bg_tasks.discard)`.
appliedFix: Module-level _bg_tasks set holds strong refs to save_to_db tasks with add_done_callback(_bg_tasks.discard).

summary: When FinBERT fails to load, analyze_sentiment returns all-zero scores before fetching any feeds, even though the geopolitical/political keyword scoring (_score_geopolitical_impact) needs no ML model; sentiment health is also absent from the /health snapshot (main.py:165-170 checks only technical+chronos).
impact: Silent degradation: with FinBERT down, world_score and india_political_score are permanently 0.0, so the macro crash penalty (main.py:504) can never fire even during a genuine geopolitical shock, and every composite loses its sentiment component with no operational signal that this happened — the fallback silently skews ranking toward pure technicals.
fix: Simpler than adding a new keyword-only path: remove the early return at sentiment.py:301 (or only skip symbol-specific scoring) and let the existing pipeline run — _score_headlines_advanced already tolerates sentiment_pipeline=None. To keep the -0.5 macro threshold reachable in degraded mode, when sentiment_pipeline is None use geo_impact at full weight (1.0) instead of the 0.6/0.4 blend at line 261 (which would yield only geo_impact*0.4 with a zero base score). Log a one-time warning on degraded operation, and add a "sentiment": {"loaded": sentiment_pipeline is not None, "healthy": ...} entry to the models dict in _build_health_snapshot (main.py ~200).
appliedFix: Removed the FinBERT-down early return so keyword scoring still runs; geo_impact used at full weight when pipeline is None; one-time degraded-mode warning; added sentiment.get_status() and a 'sentiment' entry to the /health models dict in main.py.

summary: PENDING suggestions (entry never touched) display a live P&L% computed from entryPrice as though the position were filled.
impact: `isActive = s.status === 'ACTIVE' || isPending` and `pnlFromCurrent` is computed for both, so a signal whose entry was never hit shows e.g. "+1.8%" in the P&L column right next to "Awaiting Entry" — fabricated profit on a non-existent position, exactly the kind of wrong number an investor will question.
fix: Gate the P&L on fill status: `const pnlFromCurrent = !isPending && isActive && pnlRaw != null ? ... : null;` (the existing `—` branch then renders for PENDING).
appliedFix: PENDING suggestions show '—' instead of fake P&L (gated on !isPending) and DIST column shows distance-to-entry ('TO EN') while awaiting fill.

summary: calcVwap resets its cumulative sums on the browser's LOCAL calendar day (`new Date(c.ts).getDate()`), not the IST trading day, so VWAP is wrong for any viewer outside IST.
impact: An NSE session (09:15–15:30 IST) spans local midnight for viewers in the Americas/Europe — VWAP resets mid-session and the plotted line diverges from the true session VWAP that entries/targets were derived from. Since VWAP is displayed as a trading reference line, this shows a wrong indicator value.
fix: The proposed fix is correct in principle. Slightly more robust (compares full IST calendar day, not just day-of-month, and avoids getUTCDate-only comparison): in calcVwap replace `const day = new Date(c.ts).getDate();` with `const d = new Date(Date.parse(c.ts) + 19800_000); const day = d.getUTCFullYear() * 10000 + (d.getUTCMonth() + 1) * 100 + d.getUTCDate();` (19800_000 ms = +05:30 IST offset; IST has no DST so a fixed offset is safe), keeping `prevDay` as a number initialized to -1.
appliedFix: calcVwap resets cumulative sums on the IST calendar day (ts + 19800000ms → UTC Y*10000+M*100+D key) instead of local day-of-month.

summary: "Profit Factor" is computed as avgWin/avgLoss (payoff ratio) instead of gross profit / gross loss.
impact: With 2 wins averaging ₹500 and 8 losses averaging ₹400 the panel shows Profit Factor 1.25 (looks profitable) while the true profit factor is 1000/3200 = 0.31 (heavily losing). A headline performance metric shown to investors is mathematically wrong.
fix: Compute `const grossWin = wins.reduce((s,w)=>s+Number(w.realizedPnl),0); const grossLoss = Math.abs(losses.reduce((s,l)=>s+Number(l.realizedPnl),0)); const profitFactor = grossLoss > 0 ? grossWin/grossLoss : grossWin > 0 ? Infinity : 0;`
appliedFix: Profit Factor now computed as gross profit / gross loss (avgWin/avgLoss kept only for the Avg Win/Loss display).

summary: One-per-symbol enforcement is a non-atomic check-then-insert, and ingestSignal runs concurrently from scheduler and realtime orchestrator paths.
impact: Near-simultaneous calls for the same symbol can insert duplicate open suggestions, double-counting one setup in accuracy stats and position limits.
fix: Proposed fix is directionally right; refine as: (1) add a partial unique index, e.g. CREATE UNIQUE INDEX suggestions_one_open_per_symbol ON suggestions(symbol) WHERE status IN ('ACTIVE','PENDING') — this covers both possible insert statuses; in drizzle use uniqueIndex(...).on(table.symbol).where(sql`status IN ('ACTIVE','PENDING')`). (2) In ingestSignal, chain .onConflictDoNothing() on the insert and .returning({ id: suggestionsTable.id }); if no row is returned, log and return false (also skip the subsequent broadcast/side effects that assume the insert succeeded). Note the index only enforces the open-suggestion half of the dedup; the "one per symbol per day even after close" check remains a best-effort SELECT, which matches current intent.
appliedFix: Added per-symbol keyed-promise mutex (withSymbolLock) serializing the check-then-insert across scheduler and realtime callers (both in-process), plus .onConflictDoNothing() on the insert with failure return when no row is returned. NOTE: in-process per-symbol keyed mutex applied; DB partial unique index deliberately NOT added (would need unattended migration).

summary: Rejections from ingestSignal gates (ban, demotion, internals, delivery %, R:R, gap risk, already-open) are never added to rejectionCounts, so the risk_gate/capacity_gate/execution_guard summary buckets are permanently zero.
impact: Diagnostics mislead operators investigating why no suggestions were generated, hiding which gates are actually rejecting candidates.
fix: Change ingestSignal to return a rejection reason string (or null on success) — e.g. "setup_demoted", "fno_ban", "corporate_action", "market_internals", "delivery_pct", "risk_reward" (late-entry/live-R:R gates), "already_open", "gap_risk". In the generation loop, treat null as success (invert the current `if (success)` truthiness check) and call addRejection(reason) otherwise. Also update the second caller in intelligence/orchestrator.ts:212 (it ignores the return value; ensure no truthiness check breaks). Finally, extend summarizeRejections to map the new reasons that lack buckets (setup_demoted/fno_ban/corporate_action/market_internals/delivery_pct/gap_risk → risk_gate or execution_guard as appropriate), since only "risk_reward" and "already_open" match the existing vocabulary.
appliedFix: ingestSignal returns rejection-reason string (null on success) for all gates; generation loop routes reasons into rejectionCounts; summarizeRejections extended to bucket the new reasons; orchestrator caller updated for the new return type.

summary: The 'relax VIX warn if FII buying' unpause branch is dead code: suggestionsPaused is only true when indiaVix > vixPauseThreshold, so the guard `suggestionsPaused && indiaVix <= vixPauseThreshold` can never both hold.
impact: The documented behavior (elevated VIX offset by strong FII buying un-pauses signals) never happens; suggestions stay paused in exactly the scenario the code and log message claim to handle.
fix: The proposed fix is incomplete: softening the VIX comparison inside the block won't help because line 281's `regime !== "HIGH_VOLATILITY"` guard already excludes every paused state. Either (a) delete lines 292–297 as dead code, or (b) implement the intent as a separate check after the FII block (not inside the regime-guarded block), e.g.: `if (suggestionsPaused && regime === "HIGH_VOLATILITY" && fiiNetInr !== null && fiiNetInr > 2000 && indiaVix !== null && indiaVix <= vixPauseThreshold * 1.05) { suggestionsPaused = false; pauseReason = null; logger.info(...); }` — i.e., allow strong FII buying to unpause only when VIX is marginally above the threshold.
appliedFix: Replaced the dead unpause branch with option (b) from the fix spec: a separate check after the FII block that unpauses (and clears the VIX latch) when regime is HIGH_VOLATILITY, FII > 2000, and VIX ≤ threshold*1.05.

summary: The VIX pause gate has no hysteresis: pause when vix > threshold, resume the moment vix <= threshold, so a VIX hovering at the threshold flips suggestionsPaused (and broadcasts marketRegimeChanged) on every detectRegime tick.
impact: Suggestion flow toggles on/off repeatedly during volatile sessions — precisely when stability matters — producing whipsaw entries right at the risk boundary and WS event spam to the frontend.
fix: Latch only VIX-caused pauses, not any previous pause: in Step 2's warn zone, keep suggestionsPaused=true if the system was previously paused *by the VIX gate* (e.g., previousPaused && state.pauseReason?.includes("exceeds pause threshold"), or better, track a module-level `vixPauseLatched` boolean set in Step 1 and cleared only when indiaVix <= vixPauseThreshold * 0.95). Using bare previousPaused would wrongly extend unrelated pauses (FII-selling pause line 309, geopolitical pause line 321, scheduler breaker pauses in jobs.ts) into the warn zone and misattribute pauseReason. Keep the Step-5 FII un-pause carve-out (line 293) as an intentional override that also clears the latch.
appliedFix: Added VIX hysteresis via module-level _vixPauseLatched: set on hard pause (VIX > threshold), holds the pause through the warn band (> threshold*0.95) with an explanatory pauseReason, cleared only when VIX ≤ threshold*0.95; band commented at Step 2.

summary: decisionReason fabricates an EMA comparison: `niftyChangePct >= 0` is rendered as 'NIFTY > 20 EMA' — no EMA is computed anywhere in this engine.
impact: The user-facing regime explanation is false whenever daily change sign and 20-EMA position disagree (e.g. a +0.3% bounce inside a downtrend below the 20 EMA shows 'NIFTY > 20 EMA'), undermining trust in why suggestions were made or paused.
fix: Rename the label to what is measured (`niftyChangePct >= 0 ? "NIFTY up on day" : "NIFTY down on day"`), or compute the real EMA20 relation from Nifty daily candles and use that.
appliedFix: decisionReason now says "NIFTY up on day" / "NIFTY down on day" based on niftyChangePct sign, removing the fabricated 20-EMA claim.

summary: _monitor_health awaits nothing around _refresh_health_snapshot(), which runs RuntimeDiagnostics.collect() — an nvidia-smi subprocess with a 2s timeout plus torch CUDA queries — synchronously on the event loop every 60s; /health (line 420) does the same when the snapshot is stale.
impact: Every 60 seconds the event loop can stall up to ~2s, adding latency spikes to concurrent batch inference requests during scans (visible as periodic slow suggestion refreshes).
fix: Call `await asyncio.to_thread(_refresh_health_snapshot)` in _monitor_health, and in the /health handler use `snapshot = await asyncio.to_thread(_refresh_health_snapshot)` for the stale path.
appliedFix: _monitor_health and the /health stale path now call _refresh_health_snapshot via asyncio.to_thread, keeping nvidia-smi/CUDA diagnostics off the event loop.

summary: HistoryRow labels every closed position with positive PnL as "TARGET HIT" and everything else (including breakeven, manual close, expiry close) as "STOP HIT".
impact: A position closed manually or by time-stop at a small loss is displayed as "STOP HIT", and a trailing-stop exit in profit is displayed as "TARGET HIT" — the audit trail misrepresents how trades actually exited.
fix: The close reason is not on the position record, so the fix needs backend persistence first: (1) add a closeReason column to paperPositionsTable (db/src/schema/paper_trading) or join the exit order's orderType in the history query; (2) set it to exitReason ("TARGET_EXIT"/"STOP_EXIT") in the two close transactions in backend/src/trading/paper_engine.ts (~lines 427 and 512); (3) include it in the /history response (backend/src/routes/paper_trading.ts:76-78) and add closeReason?: string to the PaperPosition interface (frontend/src/types/api.ts:224); (4) in HistoryRow, badge from closeReason when present ("TARGET HIT" / "STOP HIT", with a trailing-stop-in-profit optionally shown as "TRAIL STOP"), falling back to a neutral "CLOSED +/-" label — not TARGET/STOP — when absent (legacy rows).
appliedFix: Frontend HistoryRow badges from closeReason (TARGET_EXIT->TARGET HIT, STOP_EXIT->STOP HIT/TRAIL STOP, else honest CLOSED). Backend /paper/history now joins paperOrdersTable to attach closeReason = latest non-ENTRY orderType per suggestionId.

summary: Unsubscribe removes the callback but never deletes the per-symbol Set or the symbol's data entry, so `subscribers` and `data` Maps grow monotonically for every symbol ever rendered.
impact: In a long-running dashboard session that cycles through search results, watchlists, and suggestion symbols, hundreds of empty Sets and default SymbolData objects accumulate and `notify()` iterates dead map entries — a slow leak in the exact subscriber-set structure flagged for audit (data staleness is bounded, but memory/notify cost is not).
fix: In the unsubscribe closure only: `const set = this.subscribers.get(k); if (set) { set.delete(callback); if (set.size === 0) this.subscribers.delete(k); }`. Do NOT evict `this.data` entries — the store intentionally caches last-known-good values so remounting components never see undefined/null-flash; LRU eviction of data would break that documented contract.
appliedFix: Unsubscribe deletes the per-symbol Set when empty; data entries intentionally retained per the vetted fix (last-known-good cache contract).

summary: `expiredTrades` and `completedTrades` are computed from the 5-second-polled history query but never rendered — the panel only shows active trades.
impact: The slider refetches `/api/suggestions/history?limit=100` every 5s while open purely to compute arrays used only in an empty-state condition; users see "No signals generated yet" logic driven by data that is otherwise invisible, and the intended completed/expired track-record sections silently never appear (dead code masking missing UI).
fix: Render the missing sections after the Active Trades block, reusing SuggestionCard (whose win/loss/outcome logic already exists): add a "Completed ({completedTrades.length})" section mapping completedTrades and an "Expired ({expiredTrades.length})" section mapping expiredTrades, each gated on length > 0. Also consider relaxing the history query's refetchInterval (e.g. 30-60s) since closed history changes rarely.
appliedFix: Rendered 'Completed (n)' and 'Expired (n)' sections reusing SuggestionCard, gated on length>0; relaxed history refetchInterval 5s→30s.
